Confidentiality.name: weak_confidentiality_protection
Confidentiality.summary: The confidentiality protection may be weaker than the ACTA baseline.
Confidentiality.buyer_impact: The drafting party may have reduced recourse if confidential information is mishandled.
Confidentiality.seller_impact: The counterparty carries a lighter protection standard than ACTA typically expects.
Confidentiality.action: Use clearer confidentiality obligations and standard ACTA exceptions.

Indemnification.name: unlimited_liability
Indemnification.summary: The clause creates exposure beyond the ACTA baseline's negligence-based allocation.
Indemnification.buyer_impact: The drafting party may face uncapped indemnity or defense exposure.
Indemnification.seller_impact: The counterparty can shift broad downstream liability without a matching cap or fault standard.
Indemnification.action: Cap liability and restore mutual, negligence-based indemnification language.

Indemnification.name: one_way_indemnity
Indemnification.summary: The clause appears one-sided compared with ACTA's mutual indemnity position.
Indemnification.buyer_impact: The drafting party may absorb product or negligence risk without reciprocity.
Indemnification.seller_impact: The counterparty receives broader protection than the ACTA baseline normally provides.
Indemnification.action: Reframe indemnity as mutual and tie it to negligence or product liability.

Payment Terms.name: extended_payment_timeline
Payment Terms.summary: The payment timing is slower than ACTA's net-30 style baseline.
Payment Terms.buyer_impact: The drafting party may wait longer for reimbursement and carry performance costs.
Payment Terms.action: Restore net-30 timing and tie payment to itemized invoicing.

Publication Rights.name: publication_veto
Publication Rights.summary: The clause looks more restrictive than ACTA's limited review and patent-delay approach.
Publication Rights.buyer_impact: The drafting party may lose academic publication rights or timeline control.
Publication Rights.seller_impact: The counterparty gains a de facto veto over publication.
Publication Rights.action: Limit review rights to a short comment period and patent filing delay only.

Termination.name: unilateral_termination
Termination.summary: The clause gives one party unilateral termination rights that exceed ACTA's balanced termination framework.
Termination.buyer_impact: The drafting party may lose continuity, budget certainty, or patient safety protections.
Termination.seller_impact: The counterparty can exit without reciprocal notice or transition duties.
Termination.action: Add notice periods, patient protection steps, and post-termination obligations.

Governing Law.name: exclusive_venue
Governing Law.summary: The clause can impose a one-sided litigation burden compared with the ACTA baseline.
Governing Law.buyer_impact: The drafting party may have to litigate in an unfavorable or distant forum.
Governing Law.seller_impact: The counterparty secures procedural leverage on venue.
Governing Law.action: Negotiate a neutral venue or commercially reasonable governing-law compromise.
